user_request: Draft a civil suit for recovery of possession of immovable property. Plaintiff is owner of the property situated at Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore and Defendant is in unauthorized occupation after expiry of permissive possession/lease granted on 01.01.2020 which expired on 31.12.2022. Defendant refusing to vacate despite legal notice dated 15.03.2023. Property value is Rs.50,00,000/-. Monthly rental value is Rs.25,000/-. Seek recovery of possession, mesne profits from 01.01.2023 at Rs.25,000/- per month, and costs of the suit. File before the City Civil Court, Bangalore.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of possession of property at Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore from Defendant who is in unauthorized occupation after the expiry of a lease/permissive possession (01.01.2020 to 31.12.2022). Defendant refused to vacate despite legal notice dated 15.03.2023.

chronology.event: Cause of action arose (start of mesne profits)

Lease or permissive possession agreement dated 01.01.2020
Property title documents for Survey No. 45, Koramangala

draft_artifacts.title: Eviction Plaint
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE

AT BANGALORE

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF POSSESSION OF IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH MESNE PROFITS UNDER SECTION 5 OF THE SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT, 1963 READ WITH SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the immovable property the subject matter of this suit is situated at Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, which falls within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the cause of action arose within such territorial limits as the Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession of the said property situated within the jurisdiction of this Court.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction, which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits for recovery of possession of immovable property.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more fully described in the Schedule hereunder, having acquired valid title to the same by virtue of {{TITLE_DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}. The Plaintiff's title to the said property is clear, marketable and free from all encumbrances.

5. The Defendant is a person known to the Plaintiff and is currently in occupation of the said property. The Defendant was inducted as a tenant under a lease agreement dated 01.01.2020 (a true copy whereof is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A), whereby the Plaintiff granted permissive possession of the said property to the Defendant for a fixed term.

6. The said lease agreement dated 01.01.2020 was for a fixed term of three years commencing from 01.01.2020 and expiring on 31.12.2022. The terms of the lease were duly accepted by the Defendant, who continued to remain in possession of the said property as a tenant during the currency of the lease term.

7. On the expiry of the lease term on 31.12.2022, the tenancy of the Defendant stood determined by efflux of time under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. The Defendant's right to remain in possession of the said property ceased and determined with effect from 31.12.2022, and the Defendant became liable to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the said property to the Plaintiff.

8. Despite the determination of the tenancy on 31.12.2022, the Defendant has failed and refused to vacate the said property and deliver possession thereof to the Plaintiff. The Defendant continues to remain in unauthorized occupation of the said property from 01.01.2023 onwards without any lawful authority or justification. The Defendant is an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy stands determined and is now a person in wrongful possession of the Plaintiff's property.

9. The Plaintiff made several oral requests to the Defendant to vacate and deliver possession of the said property. However, the Defendant has been evasive and has refused to comply with the Plaintiff's legitimate demands, thereby compelling the Plaintiff to initiate legal proceedings.

10. Left with no alternative, the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated 15.03.2023 to be served upon the Defendant through registered post A.D., calling upon the Defendant to vacate and deliver vacant possession of the said property to the Plaintiff within a reasonable time (a true copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt and acknowledgment card is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B).

11. Despite receipt and acknowledgment of the said legal notice dated 15.03.2023, the Defendant has failed and refused to comply with the demands therein and continues to remain in wrongful and unauthorized occupation of the said property. The Defendant has no right, title or interest in the said property and is liable to be evicted.

12. By reason of the Defendant's wrongful and unauthorized occupation of the said property from 01.01.2023, the Plaintiff has been deprived of the use and enjoyment of the said property and has suffered loss and damage. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover mesne profits from the Defendant for the period of such wrongful occupation at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, being the fair monthly rental value of the said property.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff submits that he is entitled to recover possession of the said property under Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963. Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 provides that a person entitled to the possession of specific immovable property may recover the same in the manner prescribed by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. In the present case, the Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the said property and is entitled to possession thereof, and the Defendant's tenancy having been determined, the Plaintiff has a superior right to recover possession.

14. The Plaintiff further submits that the Defendant's tenancy has been lawfully determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, which provides that a lease of immovable property determines, inter alia, by efflux of the time limited thereby. The lease having expired on 31.12.2022, the Defendant's right to remain in possession has ceased and the Defendant is liable to deliver possession to the Plaintiff.

15. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant, being a person in wrongful possession of the said property after the determination of the tenancy, is liable to pay mesne profits as defined under Section 2(12) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. Section 2(12) defines mesne profits as profits which the person in wrongful possession of such property actually received or might with ordinary diligence have received. The Defendant is liable to account for the profits derived or derivable from the said property during the period of wrongful occupation.

16. It is further submitted that the Plaintiff has clear and marketable title to the said property and the Defendant's occupation is wholly unauthorized after the expiry of the lease term. The Defendant has no right, title or interest in the said property and cannot claim any protection under any law. No set-off or counter-claim exists in favour of the Defendant against the Plaintiff in respect of the subject matter of the present suit.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 01.01.2023 when the Defendant, despite the expiry of the lease term on 31.12.2022, failed and refused to vacate the said property and deliver possession thereof to the Plaintiff, thereby continuing in wrongful and unauthorized occupation of the Plaintiff's property.

18. The cause of action further arose on 15.03.2023 when the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice to be served upon the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to vacate the said property, and the Defendant despite receipt of the said notice failed and refused to comply with the demands therein.

19. The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to be in wrongful and unauthorized possession of the said property from day to day, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use and enjoyment of his property, and the said cause of action continues to accrue and subsist from day to day within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

20. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 67 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of twelve years from the date when the tenancy is determined for a suit by a landlord to recover possession from a tenant. The tenancy having been determined on 31.12.2022 by efflux of time, and the suit being filed within the prescribed period of twelve years therefrom, the present suit is well within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

21. The suit is valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, being the market value of the immovable property the subject matter of this suit. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

MESNE PROFITS

22. The Plaintiff claims mesne profits from the Defendant for the wrongful use and occupation of the said property from 01.01.2023 at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, being the fair monthly rental value of the said property, as the Defendant has been in wrongful possession of the said property and has deprived the Plaintiff of the use and enjoyment thereof. The Plaintiff is entitled to:

    (a) Past mesne profits from 01.01.2023 till the date of filing of this suit at Rs. 25,000/- per month;

    (b) Future mesne profits from the date of filing of this suit till the date of delivery of possession under Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, at Rs. 25,000/- per month or such other amount as this Hon'ble Court may determine upon inquiry.

PRAYER

23. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of possession of the immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more fully described in the Schedule hereunder, in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant, and direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the said property to the Plaintiff;

    (b) Pass a decree for mesne profits in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month from 01.01.2023 till the date of delivery of possession of the said property, or at such other rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable;

    (c) Award costs of this suit including court fees, advocate fees and all pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;

    (d) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

draft_artifacts.title: Suit for Recovery of Possession of Immovable Property with Mesne Profits
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL JUDGE

AT BANGALORE

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF POSSESSION OF IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH MESNE PROFITS UNDER SECTION 5 OF THE SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT, 1963 READ WITH SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the immovable property the subject matter of this suit is situated at Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, which falls within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the cause of action arose within such territorial limits as the Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession of the said property situated within the jurisdiction of this Court.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction, which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits for recovery of possession of immovable property.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more fully described in the Schedule hereunder. The Plaintiff's title to the said property is clear, marketable and free from all encumbrances.

5. The Defendant was inducted as a tenant under a lease agreement dated 01.01.2020 (a true copy whereof is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A), whereby the Plaintiff granted permissive possession of the said property to the Defendant for a fixed term.

6. The said lease agreement dated 01.01.2020 was for a fixed term of three years commencing from 01.01.2020 and expiring on 31.12.2022. The Defendant continued to remain in possession of the said property during the currency of the lease term.

7. On the expiry of the lease term on 31.12.2022, the tenancy of the Defendant stood determined by efflux of time under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. The Defendant's right to remain in possession of the said property ceased and determined with effect from 31.12.2022, and the Defendant became liable to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the said property to the Plaintiff.

8. Despite the determination of the tenancy on 31.12.2022, the Defendant has failed and refused to vacate the said property and deliver possession thereof to the Plaintiff. The Defendant continues to remain in unauthorized occupation of the said property from 01.01.2023 onwards without any lawful authority or justification. The Defendant is an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy stands determined and is now a person in wrongful possession of the Plaintiff's property.

9. Left with no alternative, the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated 15.03.2023 to be served upon the Defendant through registered post A.D., calling upon the Defendant to vacate and deliver vacant possession of the said property to the Plaintiff within a reasonable time (a true copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt and acknowledgment card is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B).

10. Despite receipt and acknowledgment of the said legal notice dated 15.03.2023, the Defendant has failed and refused to comply with the demands therein and continues to remain in wrongful and unauthorized occupation of the said property. The Defendant has no right, title or interest in the said property and is liable to be evicted.

11. By reason of the Defendant's wrongful and unauthorized occupation of the said property from 01.01.2023, the Plaintiff has been deprived of the use and enjoyment of the said property and has suffered loss and damage. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover mesne profits from the Defendant for the period of such wrongful occupation at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, being the fair monthly rental value of the said property.

LEGAL BASIS

12. The Plaintiff submits that he is entitled to recover possession of the said property under Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963. Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 provides that a person entitled to the possession of specific immovable property may recover the same in the manner prescribed by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. In the present case, the Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the said property and is entitled to possession thereof, and the Defendant's tenancy having been determined, the Plaintiff has a superior right to recover possession.

13. The Plaintiff further submits that the Defendant's tenancy has been lawfully determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, which provides that a lease of immovable property determines, inter alia, by efflux of the time limited thereby. The lease having expired on 31.12.2022, the Defendant's right to remain in possession has ceased and the Defendant is liable to deliver possession to the Plaintiff.

14. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant, being a person in wrongful possession of the said property after the determination of the tenancy, is liable to pay mesne profits as defined under Section 2(12) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. Section 2(12) defines mesne profits as profits which the person in wrongful possession of such property actually received or might with ordinary diligence have received. The Defendant is liable to account for the profits derived or derivable from the said property during the period of wrongful occupation.

15. It is further submitted that the Plaintiff has clear and marketable title to the said property and the Defendant's occupation is wholly unauthorized after the expiry of the lease term. The Defendant has no right, title or interest in the said property and cannot claim any protection under any law. No set-off or counter-claim exists in favour of the Defendant against the Plaintiff in respect of the subject matter of the present suit.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 01.01.2023 when the Defendant, despite the expiry of the lease term on 31.12.2022, failed and refused to vacate the said property and deliver possession thereof to the Plaintiff, thereby continuing in wrongful and unauthorized occupation of the Plaintiff's property.

17. The cause of action further arose on 15.03.2023 when the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice to be served upon the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to vacate the said property, and the Defendant despite receipt of the said notice failed and refused to comply with the demands therein.

18. The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to be in wrongful and unauthorized possession of the said property from day to day, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use and enjoyment of his property, and the said cause of action continues to accrue and subsist from day to day within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

19. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 67 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of twelve years from the date when the tenancy is determined for a suit by a landlord to recover possession from a tenant. The tenancy having been determined on 31.12.2022 by efflux of time, and the suit being filed within the prescribed period of twelve years therefrom, the present suit is well within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, being the market value of the immovable property the subject matter of this suit. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

MESNE PROFITS

21. The Plaintiff claims mesne profits from the Defendant for the wrongful use and occupation of the said property from 01.01.2023 at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, being the fair monthly rental value of the said property, as the Defendant has been in wrongful possession of the said property and has deprived the Plaintiff of the use and enjoyment thereof. The Plaintiff is entitled to:

    (a) Past mesne profits from 01.01.2023 till the date of filing of this suit at Rs. 25,000/- per month;

    (b) Future mesne profits from the date of filing of this suit till the date of delivery of possession under Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, at Rs. 25,000/- per month or such other amount as this Hon'ble Court may determine upon inquiry.

PRAYER

22. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of possession of the immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more fully described in the Schedule hereunder, in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant, and direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the said property to the Plaintiff;

    (b) Pass a decree for mesne profits in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month from 01.01.2023 till the date of delivery of possession of the said property, or at such other rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable;

    (c) Award costs of this suit including court fees, advocate fees and all pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;

    (d) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

All that piece and parcel of immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, situated at Koramangala, Bangalore, measuring about {{PROPERTY_AREA}} sq. ft., bounded as follows:

North: {{BOUNDARY_NORTH}}
South: {{BOUNDARY_SOUTH}}
East: {{BOUNDARY_EAST}}
West: {{BOUNDARY_WEST}}

|---------|-------------|----------|
| 1. | Lease agreement dated 01.01.2020 | Annexure A |
| 2. | Legal notice dated 15.03.2023 with postal receipt and acknowledgment card | Annexure B |

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that no part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of any other court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at Bangalore on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF
